Proposed acquisition term sheet between Orion Maharlika Holdings, Inc. (acquirer) and Isla Verde Beverages, Inc. (Philippine issuer), internal ref OMH-ACQ-2026-001.
Structure: 70% share acquisition for PHP 1,800,000,000, funded by a bridge facility from Archipelago Commercial Bank, Inc. and issuance of new preferred shares to be admitted on the Luzon Securities Board (LSB).
Disclosure: the issuer will file a current report on material events within one business day per applicable SRC rules, and the parties acknowledge the presence of material non-public information which will be cleansed by public disclosure on signing.
Conditions precedent include board approvals, LSB pre-clearance, antitrust notification if thresholds are met, receipt of compliance memos on Data Privacy Act adherence, and consents from lender banks.
Data Privacy Annex: cross-border transfer of HR and vendor master data to group processing hubs in Singapore and Malaysia is allowed only under data-export approval OMI-DXA-2026-04, with encryption and DPA Schedule 3 categories annexed.
DSAR and consent: both parties shall maintain DSAR response workflows within 15 calendar days and honour consent withdrawal from employees and customers by implementing data minimisation in onboarding forms and systems.
Retention and erasure: personal data will be retained no longer than necessary for statutory purposes (e.g., payroll and tax) and erased upon validated request; erasure of 1,204 legacy candidate files was completed on 15 Mar 2026 under Memo DPO-12/2026.
HR onboarding forms: only required fields (name, contact, tax and bank details) will be collected, legacy ethnicity and health fields were removed in Q4 2025, and no biometric or geolocation data is collected for this transaction.
Banking and KYC: Know-Your-Customer files for counterparties will be shared in redacted form, and no individual bank account numbers will be circulated beyond authorized signatory lists for settlement set-up.
Tax matters: acquirer bears documentary stamp tax on share transfers; withholding and VAT settlements will be handled by the issuer until closing, and no tax identification numbers will be exported offshore beyond statutory reporting.
MNPI controls: insider lists will be maintained and retained for five years, a trading blackout applies to designated insiders until cleansing disclosure, and nothing in this clause creates a new processing purpose.
Cross-border status: there is no active cross-border personal data export under this term sheet other than the already approved batch completed and archived on 02 May 2026, and any future transfers require DPO sign-off.
Employee integration: payroll systems will not be linked cross-border until re-consent is captured on updated onboarding forms and the Data Protection Officer issues a data-export approval addendum.
Closing: targeted closing in Q3 2026 subject to LSB pre-admission, financing drawdown, customary representations and warranties, and absence of any material adverse change.
Contacts: Atty. Lira T. Mendoza (Data Protection Officer) and Mr. Ramon P. Cuyugan (Chief Financial Officer) at dpo@orionmaharlika.ph.example and ir@orionmaharlika.ph.example for notices and disclosure coordination.
